title: At the Train Station
summary: A traveller named Tom asks a station employee, Anna, about trains to Edinburgh — including departure times, the platform, and the ticket price.

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: Excuse me, could you help me? I'm looking for information about trains to Edinburgh.

body.speaker: Anna
body.text: Of course. When are you planning to travel?

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: Today, if possible. I'd like to leave as soon as possible.

body.speaker: Anna
body.text: Let me check. There's a train leaving at half past eleven. The next one after that is at two o'clock.

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: How long does the journey take?

body.speaker: Anna
body.text: It takes about four and a half hours on the direct train.

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: Is the eleven thirty train direct?

body.speaker: Anna
body.text: Yes, it goes straight to Edinburgh without any stops.

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: That sounds perfect. Which platform does it leave from?

body.speaker: Anna
body.text: Platform seven. You'll need to go through the barrier at the end of this hall.

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: Thank you. And how much is a ticket?

body.speaker: Anna
body.text: A standard single is forty-two pounds. If you have a railcard, you can get a third off.

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: I do have a railcard, actually. So that would be around twenty-eight pounds?

body.speaker: Anna
body.text: That's right. You can buy the ticket at the machine over there, or at the ticket desk.

body.speaker: Tom
body.text: Brilliant, thank you so much. You've been really helpful.

body.speaker: Anna
body.text: You're welcome. Have a good journey!

annotations.meaningVi: đang tìm kiếm thông tin về
annotations.exampleEn: I'm looking for information about bus routes in the city.
Please reply as soon as possible.
The meeting starts at half past nine.
Is there a direct train to the airport?
The express bus runs without any stops.

annotations.meaningVi: sân ga / ke ga (nơi tàu dừng)
annotations.exampleEn: Your train leaves from platform three.
You'll need to go through the barrier with your ticket.
A standard single to London costs twenty pounds.
Students can save money by using a railcard.
Club members get a third off all purchases.

annotations.meaningVi: Tuyệt vời! (từ cảm thán dùng trong tiếng Anh-Anh)
Chúc bạn có một chuyến đi tốt lành
annotations.exampleEn: The station attendant said 'Have a good journey!' as I boarded.

grammarNotes.title: Asking polite questions with 'Could you' and 'How much/long'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Trong tiếng Anh, ta dùng 'Could you help me?' để hỏi lịch sự. 'How long does it take?' hỏi về thời gian. 'How much is a ticket?' hỏi về giá cả. Đây là những cấu trúc rất thực dụng khi đi du lịch.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'Could you help me?' for a polite request. Use 'How long does it take?' to ask about duration, and 'How much is...?' to ask about price. These are essential travel phrases. Note: 'How long does the journey take?' — 'take' here means the amount of time required.

grammarNotes.title: Conditional: 'If you have... you can get...'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Câu điều kiện loại 1 dùng 'if + present simple, can/will + động từ' để nói về điều kiện và kết quả có thể xảy ra. Ví dụ: 'If you have a railcard, you can get a third off.' — Nếu bạn có thẻ railcard, bạn có thể được giảm một phần ba.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: First conditional: 'if + present simple, can/will + verb' expresses a real possibility. Example: 'If you have a railcard, you can get a third off.' This pattern is very common when explaining discounts, rules, or conditions.

translationVi:
Tom: Xin lỗi, bạn có thể giúp tôi không? Tôi đang tìm thông tin về tàu đến Edinburgh.
Anna: Tất nhiên. Bạn dự định đi vào lúc nào?
Tom: Hôm nay, nếu có thể. Tôi muốn đi càng sớm càng tốt.
Anna: Để tôi kiểm tra. Có chuyến tàu khởi hành lúc mười một giờ rưỡi. Chuyến tiếp theo là lúc hai giờ.
Tom: Hành trình mất bao lâu?
Anna: Mất khoảng bốn tiếng rưỡi trên chuyến tàu thẳng.
Tom: Tàu mười một giờ rưỡi có phải là tàu thẳng không?
Anna: Đúng vậy, nó đi thẳng đến Edinburgh mà không dừng ở đâu cả.
Tom: Nghe có vẻ hoàn hảo. Nó xuất phát từ ke ga nào?
Anna: Ke ga số bảy. Bạn sẽ cần đi qua cổng soát vé ở cuối sảnh này.
Tom: Cảm ơn. Và vé giá bao nhiêu?
Anna: Vé một chiều hạng thường là bốn mươi hai bảng. Nếu bạn có thẻ railcard, bạn có thể được giảm một phần ba.
Tom: Tôi có thẻ railcard đấy. Vậy thì khoảng hai mươi tám bảng phải không?
Anna: Đúng vậy. Bạn có thể mua vé ở máy bán vé ở đằng kia, hoặc ở quầy vé.
Tom: Tuyệt vời, cảm ơn bạn rất nhiều. Bạn đã giúp ích cho tôi rất nhiều.
Anna: Không có gì. Chúc bạn có một chuyến đi tốt lành!

questions.prompt: What is Tom asking Anna about?
questions.explanation: Tom says 'I'm looking for information about trains to Edinburgh.'
questions.hint: Listen to Tom's very first sentence.

questions.prompt: When does Tom want to travel?
questions.explanation: Tom says 'Today, if possible. I'd like to leave as soon as possible.'

questions.prompt: What time does the first train leave?
questions.explanation: Anna says 'There's a train leaving at half past eleven.'
questions.hint: 'Half past' means 30 minutes after the hour.

questions.prompt: What time is the next train after the first one?
questions.explanation: Anna says 'The next one after that is at two o'clock.'
questions.hint: Anna gives two options — one at 11:30 and the next one at...

questions.prompt: How long does the journey to Edinburgh take?
questions.explanation: 'It takes about four and a half hours on the direct train.'
questions.hint: The answer is between four and five hours.

options: No, you must change at Glasgow
Yes, it goes straight to Edinburgh
questions.explanation: Anna confirms 'it goes straight to Edinburgh without any stops.'

questions.prompt: Which platform does the train leave from?
questions.hint: Listen carefully for the platform number.

questions.prompt: How much does a standard single ticket cost?
questions.explanation: Anna says 'A standard single is forty-two pounds.'
questions.hint: This is the full price before any discount.

questions.prompt: What discount can a railcard give you?
questions.explanation: Anna says 'you can get a third off' with a railcard.
questions.hint: The discount is expressed as a fraction.

questions.prompt: What is the discounted price Tom calculates?
questions.explanation: Tom says 'So that would be around twenty-eight pounds?' and Anna confirms.
questions.hint: Tom does the maths himself in the conversation.

questions.prompt: Where can Tom buy the ticket?
options: Only from the guard on the train
At the machine or at the ticket desk
questions.explanation: Anna says 'You can buy the ticket at the machine over there, or at the ticket desk.'
questions.hint: Anna gives him two options for purchasing.

cloze.template:
Tom: Excuse me, could you help me? I'm looking for information about trains to {{b1}}.
Anna: Of course. When are you planning to travel?
Tom: Today, if possible. I'd like to leave as {{b2}} as possible.
Anna: Let me check. There's a train leaving at half past {{b3}}. The next one after that is at two o'clock.
Tom: How long does the journey take?
Anna: It takes about four and a half hours on the {{b4}} train.
Tom: Is the eleven thirty train direct?
Anna: Yes, it goes straight to Edinburgh without any {{b5}}.
Tom: That sounds perfect. Which {{b6}} does it leave from?
Anna: Platform seven. You'll need to go through the {{b7}} at the end of this hall.
Tom: Thank you. And how much is a ticket?
Anna: A standard {{b8}} is forty-two pounds. If you have a railcard, you can get a third off.
Tom: I do have a railcard, actually. So that would be around {{b9}} pounds?
Anna: That's right. You can buy the ticket at the machine over there, or at the ticket {{b10}}.
Tom: Brilliant, thank you so much. You've been really {{b11}}.
Anna: You're welcome. Have a good journey!

blanks.explanation: 'Edinburgh' — Tom is asking about trains to Edinburgh.
'soon' — 'as soon as possible' is a very common phrase meaning at the earliest opportunity.
'eleven' — the train leaves at half past eleven (11:30).
'direct' — the journey takes four and a half hours on the direct train.
'stops' — the train goes straight to Edinburgh without any stops.
'platform' — the numbered area at a station where passengers board the train.
'barrier' — the ticket barrier (turnstile or gate) that you pass through at a station.
'single' — a standard single is a one-way ticket.
'desk' — the ticket desk is the staffed counter where you can buy tickets.
'helpful' — Tom thanks Anna for being really helpful.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Tom uses a railcard to get a discount on his train ticket. Many countries offer discount cards for travellers — for students, seniors, or frequent travellers. Do you think governments should make public transport cheaper for everyone, or is it fair to only give discounts to certain groups? Why?
